programmes.show_description: From nightmarish bad moves to our very own super-heroes, prepare to be stunned and amazed with action from the roads around the world as we've got it all Caught On Dashcam. (S5, ep 3)
Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they keep the roads safe. (S22, ep 6)

CBP questions a Canadian attempting to enter the US with three of his American family members. An officer has no time for a Swiss traveller's watch declaration. (S1, ep 22)
In Blaine, a traveller's plan to start an e-vape venture may go up in smoke and a couple returning from a Canadian casino could go bust. (S1, ep 23)

A returning American is cuffed at the Peace Arch crossing in Washington and in Detroit, a German rock band rolls straight into secondary. (S1, ep 13)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a traveller minding manners may need to mind his story instead and the Chicago canine unit knows smugglers don't play games. (S1, ep 14)

Melora: After falling in love with a woman whose species is unable to walk in 'normal' gravity, Bashir develops a technology that could free her of her wheelchair forever.
Upgrades: Anise conducts an experiment on the team members using the Atoneikan armbands which promises enhanced ability. (S4, ep 3)
The Void: Trouble looms when Voyager is pulled into an empty layer of subspace, where ships are forced to attack each other and steal supplies for survival. (S7, ep 15)
Lonely Among Us: The crew try to solve the mystery of Assistant Chief Engineer Singh's death, and the altered personalities of Lt Worf, Dr Crusher and the Captain.
A couple from Vietnam come to claim a lost suitcase but what's hidden inside will change their lives forever. Some suspicious-smelling parcels land at the Mail Centre. (S15, ep 1)
Border Force investigate when it emerges that two young women have had their plane tickets paid for by someone they don't know. (S15, ep 2)
Stay in Your Lane: The team works quickly to uncover the motive behind the murder of a seemingly innocent man. (S6, ep 3)
Hairtrigger: The team race against the clock to track down a young anti-government terrorist with a thirst for revenge.
Broken Bow, Part 1: During the first ever episode, the Enterprise crew sets out on its maiden voyage with a mission to return a wounded Klingon to his people.
Man On The Wall: In the season finale, Chinatown reflects on the aftermath of the riots. Ah Sahm has a new opportunity to go on the offensive. (S2, ep 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following Britain's police. In an eventful shift, Devon and Cornwall cops tackle the region's drug dealers and users. (S1, ep 10)
All in a Days Work: Officers deal with a driver who claims that his glasses case looks like a phone, a motorist causes mayhem on the roads and another refuses his fine politely. (S12, ep 1)
Lead Shoes: A driver blames his shoes for his excessive speed, a night time dog walk turns into chaos and a mechanic gets into a world of trouble. (S12, ep 2)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 5)

At JFK, officers discover cocaine concealed inside a Caribbean cocktail and a Chinese student hopes to get a pass from CBP after overstaying his visa. (S1, ep 24)
CBP questions a pair of real estate entrepreneurs bound for Florida. In Blaine, a Canadian traveller's US investments are cause for concern. (S1, ep 25)

A Canadian crosses the Ambassador Bridge in Detroit by mistake and her bag of medicinal marijuana may cost her. (S1, ep 15)
In New York City, a laptop belonging to a Caribbean traveller does not compute and a young Brit arrives at Detroit Metro Airport to visit an American cyber pal. (S1, ep 16)
A lovesick Lithuanian begs for mercy, customs find enough a truckload of cigarettes, and Biosecurity deal with a sneaky pest trying to hitchhike into the country. (S11, ep 1)
A Nigerian national kicks-off with Border agencies, Customs deal with a fishy parcel from Africa, and Immigration talk to a tricky DJ trying to work without a visa. (S11, ep 2)

Rules Of Acquisition: A Ferengi female, who has defied the law and disguised herself as a male, risks everything when she falls in love with Quark.
Crossroads: An incoming traveller, identified as a Jaffa priestess with whom Teal'C once had a relationship, claims to know how to destroy the Goa'uld. (S4, ep 4)
Workforce: In the first of a two-parter, Chakotay, Kim and Neelix return from an away mission to find the Voyager abandoned, with only the Doctor onboard. (S7, ep 16)
Justice: Captain Picard has a difficult decision to make when Wesley is sentenced to death for innocently violating an alien planet's customs.
Officers suspect a Brazilian man is lying to them and the truth may lay in his bag. A Chinese student in a hurry attracts the attention of Biosecurity officers. (S15, ep 3)
Things go from bad to worse for a young man when drugs are found in his bag and his fiancee is left further unimpressed by what is revealed next. (S15, ep 4)
Two Pink Lines: Bode and his fellow firefighters band together with the civilian station 42 crew to battle a monstrous fire that erupts after a plane crash. (S1, ep 12)
Crestfallen: When a private jet carrying American citizens is shot down over Europe, the Fly Team looks into the man who missed the flight. Season finale. (S1, ep 21)
Broken Bow, Part 2: Enterprise is on its first deep space mission when the Suliban kidnap the Klingon they are transporting, forcing Archer to make a detour.
